TSLA: Lucid CEO Sees Definite EV Demand Slowdown in US and Europe. Lucid Group Inc.'s interim CEO, Marc Winterhoff, acknowledges a clear deceleration in electric vehicle demand across the US and Europe, attributing it to the expiration of federal tax credits impacting Q3 purchases. Despite this slowdown, Lucid expects to produce around 18,000 EVs this year, meeting the lower end of its forecast. This comes as analysts downgrade Lucid, Rivian, and Tesla shares, anticipating an "EV winter" to continue through next year.

‘The Dream Keeps Expanding,’ Says Top Investor About Tesla Stock. A top investor, James Foord, expresses skepticism about Tesla's latest AI chip ambitions, believing the company is spreading itself too thin. While CEO Elon Musk announced plans for high-volume AI chip production, Foord argues that Tesla lacks the necessary infrastructure and financial capacity compared to industry leaders like Nvidia. He suggests Tesla needs to focus on a clearer strategy, choosing between being an EV maker or an AI/robotics platform, rather than pursuing all aggressively.

'Uber Is Cooked,' Says Ross Gerber As Waymo Hits 450K Weekly Rides — Touts $200 Million Annual Revenue For Tesla Rival. Investor Ross Gerber believes that Alphabet Inc.'s Waymo autonomous cab service poses a significant challenge to Uber Technologies Inc. Gerber highlighted Waymo's impressive growth, reporting 450,000 weekly rides and an estimated annual revenue exceeding $200 million. This comes as Tesla CEO Elon Musk also anticipates his company's Robotaxi operations in Austin to go driverless by year-end, with a broader European expansion planned for Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology.

Tesla Pushes Incentives to Lift Model Y Sales. Tesla is offering significant incentives, including 0% APR financing for six years and free upgrades, to boost Model Y sales as the year ends. This move comes as the loss of federal tax credits impacts EV affordability and aims to protect delivery volumes amidst increasing competition from Stellantis and Ford. The effectiveness of these incentives in stimulating demand will be observed in Tesla's upcoming financial results.

Tesla Stock Drops After Morgan Stanley Downgrade. Tesla shares declined after Morgan Stanley downgraded its rating for the stock, along with Rivian and Lucid, citing slower EV adoption and a challenging trading outlook. Despite the downgrade, Morgan Stanley increased Tesla's price target to $425. This dip follows a significant recovery for Tesla shares since Elon Musk refocused on the automaker.

Morgan Stanley cuts Tesla's stock rating from 'buy' for the first time in 2 years on valuation concerns. Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco downgraded Tesla's stock from "Overweight/Buy" to "Equal Weight/Hold," citing valuation concerns and a more cautious outlook on EV adoption and competition. While the price target was raised to $425, it still implies a slight downside. The firm acknowledges Tesla's progress in FSD and robotics but advises investors to await a better entry point due to high valuation and potential near-term struggles in its auto business.

10 Consumer Discretionary Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session. This article details significant options activity, dubbed "whale alerts," in ten consumer discretionary stocks. These alerts identify large-sum transactions that can signal future price movements, with comprehensive data provided for call and put options including sentiment, expiration dates, strike prices, total trade prices, open interest, and volume. The stocks highlighted include major companies like Tesla, Amazon, and Nike, among others.
TGT: Target launches NYC concept store as incoming CEO pushes style-first strategy. Target has opened a new, first-of-its-kind concept store in SoHo, New York City, prioritizing a style-first strategy under the guidance of its incoming CEO. This unique store features curated sections by local tastemakers, rotating seasonal collections, and plans to host a cafe and events in the near future. The move signifies a shift towards emphasizing design and localized experiences for the retail giant.

Why Mama's Creations Stock Surged Today. Mama's Creations stock (MAMA) surged 28% after reporting impressive third-quarter growth metrics, with revenue increasing 50% year over year to $47.3 million. This growth was driven by double-digit organic sales and the recent $17.5 million acquisition of Crown 1 Enterprises, which added $56 million in annual revenue. The company also saw improved profitability, with net income up 31.7% and adjusted EBITDA soaring 118%, and has future growth plans including national placements at Target and Food Lion.

NYSE Content Advisory: Market Update + Target Opens Newly Transformed 'Target SoHo' in New York. This article provides a market update from the NYSE, highlighting key events like the Federal Reserve's imminent interest rate decision and the IPO of Twenty One Capital. It also announces the unveiling of the newly transformed Target SoHo store, featuring a beauty bar, selfie checkout, and curated merchandise.

New Target SoHo Concept Store Focuses on Style and Design. Target Corp. has opened its transformed Target SoHo concept store in New York City, emphasizing style, design, and experiential shopping. The store features unique zones like "Curated By," "The Drop," "Broadway Beauty Bar," and a "Gifting Gondola," aiming to provide a continuously evolving assortment and immersive experience. This initiative is part of Target's broader investment in design-driven retail and its presence in New York City.

NYC SoHo Store Showcases Target’s Style & Design Vision. Target has revamped its SoHo store in New York City into a unique concept blending shopping with discovery and style, emphasizing its commitment to design. This location features curated fashion and home items, a rotating showcase of seasonal styles, a beauty bar, and interactive gifting and checkout experiences. The store's evolution through 2026 and beyond will introduce new experiential zones, activations, and programming, reflecting CEO Michael Fiddelke's vision for style and design.

NYSE Content Advisory: Market Update + Target Opens Newly Transformed 'Target SoHo' in New York. The NYSE provides a daily market update, with Kristen Scholer delivering insights as traders await the Federal Reserve's final interest rate decision of the year. Target unveils its reimagined SoHo store, featuring a beauty bar and selfie checkout, led by incoming CEO Michael Fiddelke. Additionally, Twenty One Capital Inc. goes public on the NYSE under the ticker XXI, becoming the largest Bitcoin treasury company on the exchange.
DIS: Former Apple COO Jeff Williams Nominated to Walt Disney Company Board of Directors. The Walt Disney Company has nominated former Apple Chief Operating Officer Jeff Williams to its board of directors, increasing the board's size from 10 to 11 members. Williams, who retired from Apple in July 2025, will stand for election as an independent director at Disney's 2026 annual shareholder meeting. He previously held roles such as Head of Worldwide Procurement and VP of Operations at Apple, and was central to the launch of the first iPhone and the Apple Watch project.

Disney nominates former Apple COO Williams to board. The Walt Disney Company has nominated former Apple COO Jeff Williams to stand for election as a new independent director at the company’s 2026 annual meeting of shareholders. Williams, who retired earlier this year, was instrumental in developing the Apple Watch and architecting Apple's health and fitness strategy during his nearly three-decade career. His election will expand Disney's board to 11 members, effective from the 2026 annual meeting.

Walt Disney Co Nominates Jeff Williams to Board of Directors. Walt Disney Co announced the nomination of Jeff Williams, former COO of Apple, to its Board of Directors on December 9, 2025. This move will expand the board from 10 to 11 directors in 2026, bringing Williams' technology and global operations expertise to Disney. Shareholders will vote on his election at the 2026 meeting.

The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS) taps ex-Apple COO Jeff Williams as board nominee. The Walt Disney Company has nominated former Apple Inc. executive Jeff Williams to its board of directors, expanding the board to 11 members. Williams, who previously served as Apple's COO and was instrumental in the launch of the Apple Watch, brings extensive experience in technology, global operations, and product design. His nomination is seen as a strategic move to further Disney's focus on creative storytelling and innovation.

The Walt Disney Company Earns 27 Golden Globe Nominations. The Walt Disney Company has secured an impressive 27 Golden Globe Award nominations, demonstrating a strong presence across its film, television, and streaming divisions. These nominations span various Disney brands, including ABC, FX, Disney+, Hulu, Pixar, Lucasfilm, and 20th Century Studios. The award winners will be announced on Sunday, January 11, 2026.
